title: Gently Fermented Australian Tripe Hotpot
description: A low-sodium, citrusy Australian dish featuring tripe, fermented for a crunchy texture, perfect for meal prep. This hotpot is a hearty and healthy option for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: tripe
ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions: Combine tripe, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, and ginger in a large bowl and mix well.
Cover the bowl with a lid or plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to marinate.
Remove the tripe mixture from the refrigerator and add the fermentation starter, stirring gently to combine.
Transfer the tripe mixture to a hotpot vessel and add the water, stirring gently to combine.
Cover the hotpot vessel with a lid and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour, or until the tripe is tender and the liquid has reduced slightly.
Stir in the salt-free seasoning blend and continue to simmer, covered, for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the hotpot from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Refrigerate the hotpot, covered, for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld and the tripe to ferment slightly.
